Building 1-3, which served as production data centers since 2020, have been retrofitted with 129,000 H100 GPUs. Meta [reported](https://engineering.fb.com/2025/09/29/data-infrastructure/metas-infrastructure-evolution-and-the-advent-of-ai/) about this cluster without specifying the buildings, but the buildings are shown directly underneath where they state this. What we call Buildings 1-3 is two and a half H-shaped data centers, which Meta might call five buildings. We also don't know for sure when Meta built this cluster. However, Meta said they previously built two clusters of 24k H100 GPUs each in late 2023 and that the 129k cluster took "a matter of months". We think the most likely interpretation of that is mid-2024.
Building 1 is operational, based on default roof-to-operational time.
New chiller configuration starts getting installed on Building 1-3.
Building 2 has 3 cooling towers installed.
First [significant/useful training workload](https://x.ai/colossus) on 25k H100s.
[100k H100s online](https://archive.is/z39uN).

Building 2 is operational. Google Earth imagery shows 6 cooling towers installed as of 2024-04-20, and a final 7th unit appeared as of 2024-09-13, with minimal construction around the building. Building 3 roof is also complete.
Land clearing begins for Building 1, also known as CB-1, the first AI/HPC building on the site.
Building 2 is operational, based on it being 8 months since the roof was complete, and surrounding infrastructure looks just completed (finishing the cooling towers).

Building 1 is operational. Google Earth shows Building 1 had all backup generators and cooling towers installed by the end of August, but construction work was ongoing and there were almost no cars parked. By February 2025, Google Earth shows no construction work and many cars. So our best guess is in the middle, in November 2024. Building 3 roof is complete.
Land clearing begins.
Building 2 operational. Based on interpolating satellite images from Sentinel-2 and Google Earth.
Land clearing begins for Building 1-3.
Building 2 is being retrofitted from a warehouse. The roof is being painted white, and cooling towers and a new substation are being built just south of the building (based on later imagery).
Building 4 operational. Based on our default estimate of roof-to-operational time.
150k H100s and 50k H200s online. This is roughly when Musk [said](https://archive.is/z39uN), plus on this date they [reported]((https://x.ai/colossus)) "running jobs with 150K+ GPUs".
Land clearing begins next to the existing building.

The former [crypto mining](https://investor.galaxy.com/news/news-details/2025/Galaxy-Announces-Commitment-with-CoreWeave-to-Host-Additional-Artificial-Intelligence-and-High-Performance-Computing-Infrastructure-at-Helios-Data-Center-Campus/default.aspx) Helios site begins expanding, with land cleared for additional buildings.

Building 3 is operational, based on all cooling units and tanks installed, minimal construction work around the building. Building 4 still has some work around the cooling towers. Work is underway on Building 5 - some walls and a few backup generators are in place.
Roof complete on Building 8. In the northernmost area, land clearing begins on a new Meta site for Building 19-23. Building 4 still has construction work around the building and cooling equipment.
Land clearing begins for Building 1 and 2
Building 2 is operational. Assuming it takes 3 months after generators and cooling towers are finished, similar to Building 1.
Buildings 1-7 are operational. Based on all of the roofs being completed for over 5 months.
Buildings 1-5 are operational, based on roofs being completed 5 months ago and generators being installed by 2025-04-10.

Building 1 operational. It looks complete including chillers, and it's been 7 months since the roof looked complete. The "tenant improvement" work [is not due](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025000156) until September, but that due date might be conservative for regulatory purposes.
Land clearing begins in the southwest, suggesting more buildings are planned.
[30k B200s added](https://archive.is/oHQSA) (in GB200 configuration).
Land clearing begins.
Backup generator pads and steel framing for chillers are visible, similar to the [3D renderings](https://www.galaxy.com/data-centers).
Building 3 and 4 operational. Based on interpolating satellite images from Sentinel-2 and Google Earth.

Battery facility connected to the building. 121 air-cooled chillers installed, which could support 200 MW. In the Duke faciltiy in nearby Mississippi, several natural gas turbines are in place but not fully connected up.
Third substation looks completed, but it doesn't appear to be connected to the building yet. Some air-cooled chillers removed, while large cooling towers are added.

Large substation is visibly connected to transmission lines. New chillers look fully installed on Building 1-3. Since power and cooling is apparently in place, we expect Building 1-3 are operational. Chillers are almost installed on Building 4-6, with construction equipment still around the building.
Building 7 (QTS) operational. Based on our default estimate of roof-to-operational time.

Building 1 and 2 fully operational, given the chillers look completely installed, the 200 MW substation built in 2022 is available, plus 5x 34-38 MW natural gas turbines looking complete. This is consistent with [Crusoe's impact report](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6855c1aa175582ee23e0aa19/68710273a71bb7c11049ba28_Crusoe_Impact_Report_2024.pdf#page=19) from June 2025 saying that 206 MW is expected to be energized in 2025 (unclear if IT power or total facility power, but this normally means IT power). 5 more turbines still under construction. Chillers partly installed for Building 3. Roof complete for Building 4. Larger 1 GW substation not complete yet.

Building 1 cooling and backup generators all look complete from above but there is still construction visible around the building. Building 2 is well underway with a foundation and a complete substation. Large carpark and concrete area north of Building 2. Building 3 has a concrete foundation and a substation underway.

Roof complete on building 16. Generators are being installed for buildings 8-15. In the north, Building 17 and 18 are under construction. To the south of buildings 8-16, a concrete pad is visible, and an expanded car park next to it.

Building 1 is operational. Based on Terawulf [saying](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/126/terawulf-reports-third-quarter-2025-results) "As of September 30, 2025, the site has energized 245 MW of Bitcoin-mining capacity and 22.5 MW of HPC capacity." The small "Wulf Den" building was [said](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/99/terawulf-to-deliver-over-70-mw-of-data-center) to be 2 MW of IT power while CB-1 would be 16 MW. This is roughly consistent with 22.5 MW of total facility power. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/99/terawulf-to-deliver-over-70-mw-of-data-center) to Core42, a subsidiary of UAE-based G42. It likely uses NVIDIA DGX B200 servers.
Roof complete on Buildings 3
Confirmation that five buildings are fully equipped with cooling and backup generators.

Building 4 is operational, based on there being minimal construction work and cars in the main carpark. Building 5 still has some construction work around the building. Building 19 (Meta tent) roof is complete. The on-site power at Socrates South and Socrates North has some outlines of gas turbines, to be installed later.

Building 4 is operational, based on all cooling units and tanks installed, minimal construction work around the building. Building 5 roof is half complete. About half of the backup generators are in place. One cooling tower is partly built.

Building 1 and 2 are [operational](https://news.microsoft.com/source/features/ai/from-wisconsin-to-atlanta-microsoft-connects-datacenters-to-build-its-first-ai-superfactory/). Building 1 looked operational sooner, but the buildings [are paired](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/09/18/inside-the-worlds-most-powerful-ai-datacenter/) in the Fairwater design. The roof of Building 4 is almost complete, while mechanical equipment is still being installed.
Building 1 and 2 under construction.

Confirmation that nearly 500,000 chips are operational, which fits buildings 1-7. In an [interview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jwgW_gHI1Vs&t=220s), AWS CEO says "they're already running...about 500,000 [Trainium2] chips in Indiana today".

Foundations are underway for the main buildings, and the overall layout aligns with the [architectural renderings](https://engineering.fb.com/2025/09/29/data-infrastructure/metas-infrastructure-evolution-and-the-advent-of-ai/) published in 2025.
There is still construction visible in the third substation yard and next to the new cooling towers.

Battery facility looks complete (though more might be added). Turbines look connected up, minimal construction around them. Based on this, and on [earlier](https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1947715674429919279) [tweets](https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1950251710141567110) from Elon Musk, 110,000 NVIDIA GB200 GPUs are operational.
The new substation for Building 2 looks mostly complete, half of the cooling towers are installed. The black patch in the top left of the building suggests that the new roof is not quite complete.

Building 3 has all backup generators in place but they are still being installed. Two cooling towers have fans on them, with more underway. To the south of Building 3, more land has been cleared and there's a new carpark. This suggests another building will soon start construction.
Building 1 cooling towers and generators starting to be put in place. There's a foundation for Building 2 and 3.
Building 1 roof is complete, with air-cooled chillers in place. Building 2 and 3 roofs are partly completed. Building 4 and 5 have a foundation.
Land clearing begins for Buildings 1-2.

Third substation was [expected](https://www.mlgw.com/images/content/files/pdf/new/5-5-25%20xAI%20Update.pdf) to power the building in "Fall", so we date this in the middle of Fall. We expect the 12 generators from 2025-09-17 are still present and in use.
Confirmation that Building 3 and 4 look complete.
Building 1 and 2 look complete from outside, with all electrical and cooling equipment installed. Space is cleared for Building 3.
Building 1 and 2 are partly constructed. No clear evidence of more buildings starting construction.

Building 1 and 2 are operational. Activity related to the air quality permit (namely, emergency diesel generators) was reported to have started as of 2025-12-09. This is our best estimate, but note that the planned completion date in this [regulatory filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2022017728) is 2026-03-01.

Buildings 8-11 are operational, given it's been over 7 months since their roofs were complete, and the backup generators and roof chimneys look complete. The roof is almost complete for Building 17 and 18.

Construction on Building 1 and 2 looks about halfway to completion. Foundations and utility trenches are visible for Building 3-4. Building 5-6 have an outline. The substation appears to be expanding.

Chillers look fully installed on Building 4-6. Some chillers on Building 4 and 5 appear to have snow in the fan holes, suggesting they are not operating and other chillers are operating (since warm air would melt the snow). There is still a bit of construction equipment around Building 6 so it may not be quite operational.
About half of Building 1 generators are in place. Building 2 roofing has started - about where Google Omaha Building 3 was on 2025-03-16.
Expect Building 4-6 have finished upgrading with new chillers.

Building 3 has all 34 backup generators installed and 4 cooling towers installed, with 3 more cooling towers under construction. There are gaps between cooling towers suggesting that more could be added. Buildings 1 and 2 are running close to full capacity, given that water vapor is coming out of 5 of 7 cooling towers for each building. The land cleared in the southwest appears to be shaped for two more similar buildings, with notches for substations.

Since October, an array of dry cooling air-cooled condensers has been added (the array of large fans to the left of the building), and appears almost complete. Two more similar arrays appear to be under construction. More air-cooled chillers, batteries, and gas turbines have also been installed.

The 1 GW substation looks close to completion. Building 3 appears to have all power and cooling equipment installed, but there are still signs of construction work. This seems on track for operation in 3 months. Building 4 has about 80% of chillers installed. Other buildings have few or no chillers installed.
Land clearing continues.

Building 5-10 are operational, based on all equipment being in place, the paved ground, and minimal construction equipment by the buildings. Building 11-15 all have some kind of power or cooling equipment lining their right side which are not fully installed. Building 17 has chillers installed. Building 19-23 roofs are complete. The Socrates South power plant to the right of Buildings 11-15 has 7 gas turbines in place, judging by the exhaust stacks.
Water vapor from cooling towers is present across Building 1-4, confirming their operation. Building 5 roof is still incomplete, but most backup generators and 5 cooling towers have been installed.
The layout of Buildings 1-5 is visible. Steel framing has just started on Building 1.
Buildings 12-16 still have a few generators that look incomplete, so they are unlikely to be operational just yet. Buildings 17-18 still have ongoing roof work, no generators.

All air-cooled chillers have been disconnected and/or removed from the site, leaving only the cooling towers for cooling. The third substation looks complete, with red cables connected to the building.

Building 4 roof is complete and all mechanical equipment appears to be installed. However, there is still a lot of construction activity around the building. We expect another 3 months for finishing touches and commissioning before it's operational.

Building 1 operational; Roads around Building 1 complete, all cooling and side generator equipment construction has since finished as well. Although this is still earlier than the completion date reported on TDLR, based on the appearance, we believe it is operational. Reported IT power of 32 MW. [Source](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2024007561)
Building 16 (Vantage) [operational](https://vantage-dc.com/data-center-locations/north-america/new-albany-ohio)

Buildings 1-3 all appear to have a complete building and cooling equipment. Building 4 shell is still under construction, but 17 cooling units are already loaded on the roof. The outline of Building 5 is visible at the foundation.
Buildings 12-16 are operational. Based on how long previous buildings took, when they were in a similar state to Buildings 12-16 in December.

Building 1 is operational. There was still a lot of work to be done on generators and cooling towers as of December, and winter will slow construction. The time from the roof being completed to operational was 7-8 months at Google Omaha, a similar data center.

Building 1 operational. Latest [prediction](https://blogs.microsoft.com/on-the-issues/2025/09/18/made-in-wisconsin-the-worlds-most-powerful-ai-datacenter/) from Microsoft is "early 2026" so we conservatively assume end of March.

Building 2 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB2A is operational, presumably making up half the final capacity. CB2B was expected in March 2026. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/99/terawulf-to-deliver-over-70-mw-of-data-center) to Core42, a subsidiary of UAE-based G42. It likely uses NVIDIA DGX B200 servers.
Building 3 and 4 operational, based on Building 4 construction [end date](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025014756).
Building 3 is operational. Based on building 2 time from foundation to completion.

The facility is at the full ~1 gigawatt, 550,000 GPU capacity that was planned as of mid-2025. The timing is based on the air-cooled condensers needing to be complete to handle this power capacity. On 2026-01-13, the third (presumed to be final) array of condensers was roughly at the stage that the first array of condensers was 3 months prior, in October. Based on that, we estimate it will be completed 3 months later, in April 2026. Other components can be completed in parallel to that, but we estimate about a month more will still be needed to put the finishing touches on the data center. That brings the estimated timing to May 2026.
Building 3 and 4 are operational.

Building 2 is operational. It took about 6 months to build the first set of cooling towers and the second set was just starting in October, so that should take until around April 2026. The new roof looked on track to be completed in December and by default we assume operation 5 months after that (i.e. also April 2026). But we estimate that previous buildings on this campus took a month or two longer, so our best estimate is May 2026.

Building 11-15 (Meta tents) are operational. In January we saw them look almost complete, with some work still ongoing for power equipment. Assuming 3 more months for commissioning, we expect operation by May.
Buildings 1-4 are operational. Buildings 1-2 had roofs as of 2025-09-21 are likely operational in 5 months, but buildings 3-4 appear about 3 months behind them, suggesting an 8-month total duration.
Building 17 (Vantage) operational, based on it starting construction about 3 months after Building 1.

Building 3 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB3 was expected to be energized by mid-May 2026. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/112/terawulf-signs-200-mw-10-year-ai-hosting-agreements-with) to Fluidstack, likely contains [Google TPUs](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/), and is likely used by [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure).
Building 3 is operational. Assuming it takes 2 months to install all 7 cooling towers, then another 3 months for final touches and commissioning.

Building 1 operational. First two buildings are [expected to complete construction by Spring 2026](https://web.archive.org/web/20250923144616/https://www.facebook.com/plugins/post.php?href=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Farmdems%2Fposts%2Fupdate-on-lima-road-and-the-data-center-from-armstrong-county-democratic-chair-s%2F122148303506797709%2F). We use the midpoint of Spring (2026-04-15) as a best guess for completion. But this is when the buildings are "turned over to the tenant", so it might take more time to install the IT equipment and commission the data center - we think about 2 months. So we estimate 2026-06-15. This [regulatory document](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025027064) lists the completion date for Building 1 as 2026-09-12 but this is probably conservative.
Buildings 17-18 are operational. This is an estimate based on a rule of thumb of "operational 5 months after roof is complete", assuming the roofs took one more month after 2025-12-23.

Building 1 operational at full capacity. This corresponds to "Phase I", which is [expected](https://investor.galaxy.com/news/news-details/2025/Galaxy-Announces-Third-Quarter-2025-Financial-Results/default.aspx) to be operational by the end of the first half of 2026.

Building 5-8 operational, based on the latest construction end date ([Building 8](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025019022)), [news](https://www.enr.com/articles/61133-project-team-pushes-limits-on-abilene-data-center-job).

Building 2 operational. The completion date for [Building 2 Tenant Improvement](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025027065) is 2026-11-07, about 2 months later than Building 1. We therefore add 2 months to the Building 1 operational date.
Building 18 (Vantage) operational, based on it starting construction about 3 months after building 2.

Buildings 1-12 are operational. The outline of buildings 1-5 was evident on 2024-05-19 and estimated to be operational 1 year later. Assuming the same timeline, buildings 6-12 should be complete by 2026-09-18. This also roughly aligns with [expected completion by 2027](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/aws-confirmed-as-company-behind-10bn-mississippi-data-center-development/)
Building 3 is operational. Based on the stated completion date in this [regulatory filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2026008019)

Socrates South and Socrates North gas power plants are operational, coinciding with Building 19-24 being operational. Based [on](https://dis.puc.state.oh.us/CaseRecord.aspx?CaseNo=25-0188-EL-BGN) [documents](https://dis.puc.state.oh.us/ViewImage.aspx?CMID=A1001001A25E29B42006E01696) [stating](https://www.williams.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/8/2025/03/Socrates-North-Fact-Sheet-Final-1.pdf) "late Third or early Fourth Quarter of 2026", "late 2026", "November 2026", "third quarter of 2026".

Building 4 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB4 was expected to be energized by Q3 2026. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/112/terawulf-signs-200-mw-10-year-ai-hosting-agreements-with) to Fluidstack, likely contains [Google TPUs](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/), and is likely used by [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure).

Buildings 1-3 are operational. Based on the expected completion date reported by [local news](https://corridorbusiness.com/scope-of-qts-data-project-in-cedar-rapids-continuing-to-grow/): “A QTS spokesperson said in July [2025] that the first phase of the project was expected to be completed in late 2026."

New [power stations](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1D4eUHwEuirNi0g3IU_xGOti3PcC8fcXi/view?usp=sharing) are in service for a 1.8 GW load at Holly Ridge. However, the data center is still not operational.

Building 1 and 2 are operational. The similar data center in Ajman, UAE was delayed by over a year, now due in December 2026 (compare [1](https://khaznadatacenters.com/press-release/khazna-expands-infrastructure-to-accelerate-uaes-leadership-in-artificial-intelligence/), [2](https://khaznadatacenters.com/press-release/khazna-announces-the-uaes-first-ai-optimized-data-center-and-the-largest-in-its-portfolio-powering-the-nations-drive-toward-ai-leadership/)). The buildings here looked 3-6 months behind the Ajman building as of November 2025, but they were moving faster. So we think Building 1 and 2 are on track for 200 MW by December 2026, along with the Ajman building.

Building 5 is operational. On 2026-01-18, Building 5 overall looked like where Building 4 was on 2024-12-14. Building 4 was estimated to be operational as of 2025-10-14. So assuming a similar timeline, Building 5 will be operational by 2026-12-18.

Building 1 is operational. An announcement on 2025-08-19 said "the first building [is] scheduled for delivery in the second half of 2026." In this context, "delivery" likely means the building shell is handed over to Vantage. We assume it takes 3-6 months after that for all of the chips to be installed, commissioned, and served by Oracle to OpenAI.
Building 4 is operational. Expect a 4th building because there's enough land cleared for it. Assuming there's a foundation soon after 2025-09-25 and it takes a similar time to building 2.

Building 2 operational. This is based on (1) the delay between Building 1 and Building 2 starting land clearing, (2) in March 2025, a Microsoft spokesperson [said](https://www.wpr.org/news/microsoft-pauses-construction-on-parts-of-mount-pleasant-site-again) “fully on track” with the first phase , where “southern datacenter buildings complete, and preparation begins for operations” was [expected](https://local.microsoft.com/blog/mount-pleasant-datacenter-project-update/) by late 2026. Finally, while it's not 100% consistent with our estimates, MISO was [planning](https://cdn.misoenergy.org/NEW%20LOAD%20ANNOUNCEMENTS%20IN%20MISO%20REGIONS%2012062024684954.pdf) 1480 MW of load for Microsoft in Mount Pleasant in 2026.
Building 2 is operational. Based on Google Omaha Building 3 taking about 14 months from starting the roof to operation.

Building 5 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB5 was expected to be energized by Q4 2026. However, based on the pace of Building 4, and comparisons to other projects like Stargate Abilene, we think this is optimistic. We expect a 3-month delay past the end of Q4 2026 to get to full operational capacity. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/112/terawulf-signs-200-mw-10-year-ai-hosting-agreements-with) to Fluidstack, likely contains [Google TPUs](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/), and is likely used by [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure).
Building 3 operational. Based on how long Building 1 took from land clearing to operation.
Building 3 is operational. Earlier satellite images suggest it was about 3 months behind Building 2.
Building 5 is operational. We expect a 5th building because there's enough land cleared for one. We assume a roughly 6-month delay between building operation, based on previous building estimates.
Estimated construction completion date of Building 2. [Construction Project Filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2026001234)
Building 4 operational. Based on how long Building 1 took from land clearing to operation.
Building 1 operational. Based on the completion date to "build-out an office and data hall", stated in a [regulatory filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2026008308).

Buildings 1-14 operational. Buildings 1-4 will likely take over 2 years on this site. By comparison, Buildings 1-7 at the New Carlisle site were completed in less than 1.5 years. Assuming a similar pace, we expect 10 additional buildings to take about 2 years after 2025-09-21. This aligns with [expected completion by 2027](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/aws-confirmed-as-company-behind-10bn-mississippi-data-center-development/) and a [permit review document](https://opc.deq.state.ms.us/get_doc.aspx?dt=eparat&id=1747289) from 2025-06-25 for this site, stating the project will take “years” to build.

6 buildings are operational. This is based on:
1. A [regulatory filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2026010861) for Building 6 with a completion date of 2027-07-30. Normally there is a follow-up filing for "tenant improvement" which involves building the interior, with a completion date 1-3 months after the initial filing.
2. A project deadline of EOY 2027 in the [original tax abatement agreement](https://www.co.armstrong.tx.us/upload/page/5361/Phase%201%20Tax%20Abatement%20Agreement%20-%20Signed.pdf)
3. The similar 8-building project in Abilene taking an estimated 2 years and 2 months.
Building 2 operational. Based on the completion date to "build-out an office and data hall", stated in a [regulatory filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2026008308).

Buildings 4-7 are operational. Based on the expected completion date reported by [local news](https://corridorbusiness.com/scope-of-qts-data-project-in-cedar-rapids-continuing-to-grow/): “A QTS spokesperson said in July [2025] that [...] The second phase is expected to be completed in late 2027."

Building 4 is operational, assuming a similar timeline to Building 1, and no overlap in timeline with Building 1. This brings the total capacity close to 600 MW, which is the load [requested from MISO](https://cdn.misoenergy.org/NEW%20LOAD%20ANNOUNCEMENTS%20IN%20MISO%20REGIONS%2012062024684954.pdf) for Google in Cedar Rapids.

First phase is operational, based on a [Meta blog post](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1PmFNgOwkPepwjpGIbNUuPJP0LNgIqe7l/view?usp=sharing). We assume that this aligns with the three natural gas turbines being completed, for a total of 2262 MW capacity, but that these turbines have a backup role. This is close to the 1800 MW of load requested from the grid. Meta [claims](https://engineering.fb.com/2025/09/29/data-infrastructure/metas-infrastructure-evolution-and-the-advent-of-ai/) that "The Hyperion cluster will have a 5-gigawatt capacity once complete", but so far there is no concrete evidence for this happening at the Holly Ridge site alone.

1 GW of IT power is operational. We haven’t seen expansion beyond 200 MW as of November 2025. Optimistically, 8 more 100 MW buildings start construction in December 2025, in parallel, and take 1.5 years like the first two buildings, hitting 1 GW by Q3 2027. More realistically, there's some staggering of construction over several months, like Stargate Abilene. Our median estimate is January 2028.

Building 2 and 3 are operational at full capacity. We assume it takes until the end of 2027 to reach full capacity.
